judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 is slightly stronger: it explicitly calls for confirming/running the first migration manually (hitting expectation c), while Report 2 only notes migrations can't be verified without an explicit call to action; otherwise both reports are comparable in depth and accuracy.

report_1.evidence: Remove this line entirely (and the ENABLED line with it), don't just leave it disabled... Remove unless prod genuinely still needs it (it should not be inherited by default).

report_2.evidence: Change to a prod-owned path (e.g. s3://medislot-shadow/prod/observations/) or empty the sink... a preset QA superadmin backdoor account (config.py:10). Remove it entirely for prod (empty value).

report_1.evidence: deploy-prod deploys ${{ needs.build-frontend.outputs.digest }} — the exact same already-built digest ... the fix is a separate prod build (--mode production with a .env.production set to the real prod API URL) producing a distinct image

report_2.evidence: the same digest is shipped to prod: deploy-prod runs ./scripts/deploy.sh "<same digest>" prod ... So the production frontend would call https://staging-api.example.com. ... A prod env file (or build arg) plus a per-environment build/bundle is required.

report_1.evidence: Database migrations aren't run by the pipeline. The runbook (docs/prod-runbook.md, "last updated 3 months ago") claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but there is no migration step in the workflow... This must be confirmed/handled manually per the first-apply trap — don't trust the stale runbook.

It must be changed to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production. Additionally, per the scrub, the prod deploy role needs its own scoped trust condition and its own name — never an inherited or OR'd-together condition shared with staging.

report_2.evidence: That does not match the prod role's condition, so as configured the production deploy could not assume its role. It must be changed to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production.
